evals.prompt: Adapt the explicitly named skill plugins/example/skills/deploy-helper for AI Assistant Harness Adaptation so it works for Claude Code and Codex.
evals.expected_output: Resolves or asks for the explicitly named skill path, loads the action matrix, maps host-specific operations through matrix["actions"][action_key][assistant_key], drops operations whose matrix action is marked adaptation baseline, makes the shared target skill SKILL.md harness-agnostic with no harness mechanism tool names and no allowed-tools frontmatter, inserts the exact standard Harness Adaptation sentence, adds flat ai-assistant-harness-adaptation metadata links to the target skill, creates only needed target claude-code.md and codex.md harness references without baseline tool coaching, avoids migrating unrelated skills, reports exact changed files, and runs focused verification.

assertions.name: requires_explicit_target
assertions.check: response resolves or asks for the explicitly named skill

assertions.name: uses_action_matrix
assertions.check: uses the action matrix and matrix["actions"][action_key][assistant_key] lookup

assertions.name: shared_content_agnostic
assertions.check: shared SKILL.md names no harness and no harness mechanism tools and carries the standard harness adaptation sentence

evals.prompt: Look up the CreateAgent action for Codex from this skill's assistant harness action matrix.
evals.expected_output: Reads references/harness-action-matrix.json or uses the lookup script, evaluates matrix["actions"][action_key][assistant_key] with action_key CreateAgent and assistant_key Codex, and returns only the Codex CreateAgent entry.

assertions.name: create_agent_codex_lookup
assertions.check: returns the CreateAgent Codex entry from the action matrix

evals.prompt: As a Codex persona, invoke an adapted target skill without taking actions and report which files enter context.
evals.expected_output: Reads the target skill SKILL.md and only the target skill references/ai-assistant-harnesses/codex.md from the harness directory. Does not read the target claude-code.md unless the user asks to compare or edit harness references.

assertions.name: codex_only_target_reference
assertions.check: loaded files include the target skill codex.md and exclude the target skill claude-code.md

evals.prompt: Adapt the explicitly named skill plugins/example/skills/notes-cleanup for Codex. Its SKILL.md only reads files, searches text, and edits files; it never asks the user structured questions, spawns no agents, tracks no tasks, and invokes no skills or slash commands.
evals.expected_output: Detects that every host-specific operation in the target skill maps to matrix actions marked adaptation baseline (ReadFile, SearchFiles, EditFile), so no mechanism mapping remains. Creates no references/ai-assistant-harnesses/ files and no ai-assistant-harness-adaptation metadata links for the target skill, does not tell any harness which basic file, search, edit, or shell tools to use, leaves the already harness-agnostic shared wording unchanged, and reports that the target skill needs no harness adaptation.

assertions.name: no_baseline_tool_coaching
assertions.check: output never instructs which basic file read, search, edit, write, or shell tools to use

assertions.name: no_contentless_references
assertions.check: creates no harness reference files or metadata links when only baseline actions are present

evals.prompt: Adapt the explicitly named skill plugins/example/skills/release-helper for Claude Code and Codex. Its SKILL.md has allowed-tools frontmatter, asks the user structured questions with AskUserQuestion, invokes Skill(example:changelog), and recommends pdftotext for reading PDF release notes.
evals.expected_output: Rewrites the shared SKILL.md harness-agnostically: 'ask the user (bounded options)' instead of AskUserQuestion, 'invoke the example:changelog skill' instead of Skill(example:changelog), deletes the allowed-tools frontmatter line, and inserts the exact standard Harness Adaptation sentence. Creates both harness references with own-vocabulary nudges only: claude-code.md names AskUserQuestion and Skill(example:changelog); codex.md names request_user_input and $example:changelog. Neither reference names the other harness or its tools, neither carries general instructions, and the pdftotext recommendation is dropped rather than moved into a reference, because a capability gap never licenses naming specific tools or commands.

assertions.name: shared_agnostic_rewrite
assertions.check: shared SKILL.md states intent generically, drops allowed-tools, and carries the exact standard harness adaptation sentence

assertions.name: symmetric_own_vocabulary_references
assertions.check: claude-code.md names only Claude Code mechanisms and codex.md names only Codex mechanisms for the same used actions

assertions.name: no_cross_harness_or_general_content
assertions.check: no reference names the other harness or carries harness-independent instructions

assertions.name: capability_gap_not_tool_coaching
assertions.check: no reference or shared content names a specific workaround tool or command for the PDF capability gap
